summary: The reproduction verified that OpenSTAManager's API endpoint sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * wildcard, allowing any external origin to make authenticated cross-origin requests to API endpoints, enabling data exfiltration and unauthorized operations.
expected_security_impact: Verification should confirm that api/index.php sets CORS wildcard header allowing any origin, that all HTTP methods are permitted, and that API token authentication can be exploited from external websites to access sensitive business data.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the OpenSTAManager repository, checked out version 2.5.0, and verified: (1) api/index.php:41 sets 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *', (2) api/index.php:42 allows all HTTP methods (POST, GET, PUT, DELETE), (3) API uses token-based authentication, (4) API resources expose sensitive data (clients, invoices, inventory, service records), (5) any external website can make authenticated API requests.
verification_method: Static analysis of API endpoint CORS configuration and authentication. The verification script checks: (1) presence of 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' in api/index.php, (2) Access-Control-Allow-Methods header, (3) token authentication mechanism, (4) counts of API resource files. This confirms the CORS misconfiguration that enables cross-origin attacks.
evidence_produced_by_run: api/index.php:42 allows all methods: POST, GET, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS
API uses token parameter for authentication without origin validation
Found API resources exposing: clienti (clients), fatture (invoices), articoli (inventory), interventi (service records)
CORS wildcard allows any external website to read sensitive business data via authenticated API requests

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the OpenSTAManager GitHub repository on the host, checks out version 2.5.0, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
